summary: Fire‑filled Frost show with blazing New Minglewood & monster second set
blurb: The band rolls out a tight first set highlighted by New Minglewood Blues and Little Red Rooster, then builds into a monster second set with seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and an epic Playing In The Band → Crazy Fingers → Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance sequence. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent’s keyboards sparkle, and the rhythm section drives the energy to a soaring encore.
review: On October 10, 1982 at Frost Amphitheatre the Dead delivered one of those rare nights that feels both relaxed and explosively driven. The first set kicks off with a scorching New Minglewood Blues organ solo from Brent, followed by tight renditions of Sugaree, Little Red Rooster and a soulful Tennessee Jed. By the time they launch into China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider the band is in full groove, setting up a second‑set that never lets up – Playing In The Band rolls straight into a blistering Crazy Fingers, which segues into Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance and then rockets through Touch of Grey, The Wheel, Throwing Stones and a rousing encore of Satisfaction → It’s All Over Now Baby Blue. Jerry is “on top of his game,” delivering powerful solos on Loser, Touch of Grey and the closing Throwing Stones; Phil lays down booming bass “bombs” that drive the jams, while Brent is literally on fire with organ, piano and vocal contributions. Both Mickey and Bill lock in a propulsive rhythmic foundation that keeps the energy high from start to finish. The crystal‑clear 24‑bit soundboard capture (gd1982-10-10.sbd.fixed.miller.110783.flac24) showcases every nuance, making this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across all recordings – multiple reviewers call the band "on fire", praise Jerry's guitar, Brent's keyboards and the seamless segues (China>Rider, Crazy Fingers>Lost Sailor>Saint). No consistent negative comments about performance; only one recording notes technical issues unrelated to playing.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear 24‑bit soundboard capture with excellent balance, minimal hiss and full detail of the band’s fire, making it the definitive listening source.

key_highlights: Brent’s fiery organ solo on New Minglewood Blues sets a high‑octane tone.
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition powers the second set.
Epic Playing In The Band into Crazy Fingers → Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance sequence showcases the band at peak improvisational flow.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is on top of his game – superb tone, powerful Loser solo, commanding Touch of Grey and Throwing Stones leads, with occasional vocal weakness but overall stellar guitar work.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s low‑frequency bass drives the band; especially present on early songs and jam sections like China>Rider, laying down booming "bass bombs" that anchor the improvisations.
band_performance.Bob: Weir adds extra cheese points with vocal flourishes, spoken‑word bits and crowd‑pleasing banter; his rhythm guitar and occasional lead lines keep the music grounded.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland is literally on fire – standout organ solo on New Minglewood Blues, strong piano work in Far From Me, vibrant vocals on Little Red Rooster and creative keyboard colors throughout the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming shines in the closing set and encore, providing explosive fills and a solid rhythmic drive that fuels the band’s energy.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann locks in tight grooves, especially during the high‑energy second set, delivering powerful backbeat that propels songs like Crazy Fingers and Throwing Stones.
